figures.caption: Fig. 7: Ablation of Intermediate Feature Aggregation on THuman 2.1. Left: Distribution of localized LPIPS differences on 64× 64 foreground patches (a positive difference indicates superior perceptual quality using intermediate features). Right: Qualitative comparisons corresponding to patches from the high-performance tail of the distribution. The inclusion of intermediate features successfully recovers high-frequency textures (e.g., the camouflage pattern and jersey numbers) that are blurred by the baseline model.

Fig. 3: Intermediate Feature Aggregation Module. Feature tokens are extracted from four uniformly spaced depths of the transformer encoder. These 1D tokens are reshaped into 2D spatial feature maps, projected to a consistent hidden dimension via parallel 1 × 1 convolutions, concatenated, and fused. A progressive upsampling stage quadruples the spatial resolution to produce a dense, high-resolution feature map for vertex-aligned sampling.
Fig. 6: Ablation Study: Impact of Global Token (GT) Across View Counts on THuman 2.1
Fig. 5: Qualitative Generalization on AvatarReX. We visualize the animation capabilities on unseen subjects. Left: Input reference view. Right: Re-enactment results driven by a novel motion sequence.

Fig. 4: Explicit Animation Pipeline. Once the canonical Gaussians are predicted, we apply Linear Blend Skinning (LBS) using the target pose transforms and SMPLX weights. The posed Gaussians are then rasterized to produce the final frame. This process requires no neural network inference.

Fig. 2: Overview of the HumanGS architecture. Our model processes sparse input images and pose maps through a Transformer Encoder to extract global 2D feature maps. We then perform vertex-aligned sampling: projecting canonical SMPLX [34] vertices onto these feature maps to obtain local geometry-aware features. A lightweight Vertex Query Decoder takes these features and predicts attributes for 3D Gaussian primitives in the canonical T-pose space. This explicit asset is then passed to our rendering module for animation.
